potential_reason: Flag begins ~2 months after the registered COD (2012-09-22) and spans 2.7 years at q50r 0.35 while measured against full 149.6 MW. Midtfjellet was built in stages (Phase 1 ~2012, Phase 2 completed mid-2013, later expanded toward 150 MW), so installed capacity ramped up over this window while the power-curve baseline assumes the full fleet. Most likely a phased-commissioning data artifact (denominator > turbines actually online) rather than a real loss event.
source_name: Power Technology / Global Energy Monitor - Midtfjellet phased build
evidence_note: Project commissioned in phases (Phase 1/2 ~110 MW, later expanded to ~150 MW); capacity ramp explains sustained sub-expected output measured against full nameplate.

potential_reason: 1.7-year period at q50r 0.42, q90r 0.69. Following the phased build the fleet should be complete by 2015, so a sustained ~40% shortfall over almost two years more likely reflects a capacity/metering/weather (ERA5) mismatch in the power-curve baseline than a genuine multi-year loss. Recommend checking metered vs modelled denominators for this windfarm.
evidence_note: No specific outage/curtailment event found for this window; magnitude+duration on a mature fleet points to baseline/metering mismatch.

potential_reason: Winter period, q50r 0.67 (mild shortfall, near q90r 0.72). Consistent with seasonal icing losses and/or partial turbine unavailability over a Norwegian winter at 600 m elevation. Magnitude is modest, so a partial-availability/icing effect is more likely than a hard outage.
source_name: NVE - Norwegian wind power production data
evidence_note: Winter timing + modest ratio suggests icing or partial maintenance; no specific incident located.

potential_reason: Summer period, deep shortfall (q50r 0.27) but q90r 0.68 indicates production still tracks at high winds - a signature of one or more turbines offline (reduced fleet) during a maintenance season. Likely scheduled summer servicing / turbine outage on a subset of the 44 turbines.
evidence_note: Summer maintenance season; q50r low / q90r moderate pattern typical of partial fleet outage.

potential_reason: Spring 2025 shortfall (q50r 0.35). Spring/summer is the Norwegian maintenance window; partial turbine unavailability or component campaign on the Nordex fleet is the most likely cause. Could also include spring grid/balancing curtailment in NO5.
source_name: ENTSO-E Transparency - Unavailability of Generation Units
evidence_note: No specific 2025 incident found; spring maintenance season + moderate ratio. Check ENTSO-E outage postings for NO5.

potential_reason: Summer 2025, deepest of the pair (q50r 0.22, q90r 0.65). Sustained summer shortfall with output still rising at high wind strongly suggests several turbines out for maintenance/repair through the service season. Consider also low-summer-wind baseline error.
source_name: ENTSO-E Transparency - Unavailability of Generation Units
evidence_note: Summer outage pattern; verify against ENTSO-E unit unavailability and operator maintenance notices.

potential_reason: Very small farm (4.3 MW, ~1 turbine) only ~1 year past COD (2023-09-20). A 6-month winter-spring period at q50r 0.27 on a single/few-turbine site most plausibly reflects an extended turbine outage or fault, possibly winter icing on the Sunnmore coast (62.8N). On a one-turbine site any downtime collapses the ratio.
evidence_note: Tiny capacity = single turbine outage dominates; winter timing also consistent with icing.

potential_reason: Documented blade defects: faults were discovered on Odal turbines starting early 2023 and by April 2024, 15 of 34 turbines were out of service due to blade production defects. This winter period (q50r 0.37) falls squarely in that outage window - clear turbine unavailability from manufacturing defects.
source_name: NRK / Wind-Watch - 15 of 34 Odal turbines out of operation
evidence_note: NRK reported 15/34 turbines offline by April 2024 from blade defects discovered 2023; directly explains the shortfall.

potential_reason: Continuation of the same blade-defect outage campaign - deepest shortfall (q50r 0.19) coincides with the period when up to 15 of 34 turbines were reported out of service in 2024. Strongly attributable to ongoing blade repairs/replacements.
source_name: NRK - I Nord-Odal 15 av 34 vindturbiner ute av drift
evidence_note: 2024 blade-defect outage of nearly half the fleet matches the q50r 0.19 shortfall window.

potential_reason: Short winter period (q50r 0.39 but q90r 0.93 - production nearly matches expected at high wind). The high q90r indicates the farm runs fine in strong wind, so the shortfall is concentrated in low/icing conditions - winter icing losses on this exposed coastal site (62.2N, Stadlandet) is the most likely cause.
evidence_note: q90r 0.93 rules out a hard outage; pattern fits cold-month icing losses.

potential_reason: Spring-summer period at q50r 0.47, q90r 0.77. On a small 21 MW site (~6 turbines) a sustained warm-season shortfall through the maintenance window points to one or more turbines out for service. Some baseline error from low summer winds also possible.
source_name: ENTSO-E Transparency - Unavailability of Generation Units
evidence_note: Maintenance-season shortfall on a small fleet; verify unit outages on ENTSO-E.

potential_reason: Late-winter period, mild shortfall (q50r 0.52, q90r 0.78). Consistent with recurring winter icing losses (same site shows a 2023 Feb flag), so an icing/partial-availability seasonal effect is most likely rather than a major fault.
source_name: NVE - Norwegian wind power production data
evidence_note: Recurring late-winter pattern on an exposed coastal site implies icing-related losses.

potential_reason: Summer 2025 shortfall (q50r 0.46). Summer maintenance season on a small fleet; one or more turbines offline for servicing is most likely. Could also reflect low-summer-wind baseline overestimate.
source_name: ENTSO-E Transparency - Unavailability of Generation Units
evidence_note: Summer service-window shortfall; no specific incident located.

potential_reason: Raggovidda sits in Ost-Finnmark (70.75N), the most grid-constrained region in Norway. Statnett and operator (Varanger Kraft) have repeatedly documented that lack of grid capacity caps output and blocks further build-out until 420 kV reinforcement reaches Eastern Finnmark. The flag's identical q50r=q90r (0.58) - a flat cap across all wind speeds - is the classic signature of an export/grid limit rather than wind variability. Most likely transmission-bottleneck curtailment.
source_name: Statnett - Vindkraft i Ost-Finnmark (grid constraint report)
evidence_note: Well-documented Finnmark grid bottleneck; q50r==q90r==0.58 flat cap is textbook curtailment signature.

potential_reason: Raudfjell (part of Kvitfjell/Raudfjell, completed Oct 2020) was in its first full year of operation. The summer-2021 period at very deep q50r 0.17 (but q90r 0.62) indicates large parts of the fleet still ramping/being commissioned and tuned during the early operating period in a harsh Arctic site known for construction and operational challenges. Early-operation ramp-up / partial availability.
source_name: POWER Magazine - Norwegian Wind Farm Presents Construction, Operational Challenges (Kvitfjell/Raudfjell)
evidence_note: Project completed late 2020; documented commissioning/operational difficulties; first-year ramp explains deep summer shortfall.

potential_reason: Summer-autumn 2022, q50r 0.36 but q90r 0.93 (runs near-normal in strong wind). The high q90r argues against a grid cap and points to partial fleet unavailability during the maintenance season at this challenging Arctic site (low overall capacity factor ~28% reported). Turbine downtime / servicing most likely.
evidence_note: q90r 0.93 indicates non-grid cause; reported chronic low availability supports partial turbine outage.

potential_reason: Summer maintenance period (q50r 0.36, q90r 0.76). Roan is part of the Fosen Vind complex; summer servicing with several of its 71 turbines offline is the most likely explanation. The Fosen Sami legal dispute (Supreme Court Oct 2021 ruled licenses invalid) did NOT stop operation, so curtailment is unlikely the cause here.
evidence_note: Summer service-window shortfall; Fosen kept operating through the legal dispute so this is operational, not curtailment.

potential_reason: Spring-summer 2024 deep shortfall (q50r 0.25, q90r 0.69). Maintenance-season partial fleet outage most likely. Timing follows the March 2024 Fosen settlement; no production halt was part of that deal, so operational/maintenance downtime rather than curtailment.
evidence_note: Spring maintenance window; no curtailment mandated by the Fosen settlement.

potential_reason: q50r 0.001 (essentially zero output) while q90r 0.85 - a near-total production halt for a sustained period. On a large 255.6 MW farm this signals a major outage event: substation/grid connection fault, mass turbine stop, or a metering/data dropout. Most likely a site-wide outage; rule out a data gap.
source_name: ENTSO-E Transparency - Unavailability of Generation Units
evidence_note: Near-zero q50r over 2 months on a large farm = site-wide outage or data dropout; verify against ENTSO-E/metering.

potential_reason: q50r 0.011 (near zero) for 4 months. Such a prolonged near-total absence of output on a 255 MW operating farm is more consistent with a metering/data-feed dropout or a major prolonged outage than normal operation. Following the 2024-10 zero-output flag, a recurring data/metering problem is plausible. Flag for data validation first.
evidence_note: Repeated near-zero output windows on a healthy large farm strongly suggest a data/metering gap; confirm before treating as real loss.

potential_reason: Flag starts ~2 months after COD (2015-10-01) on a tiny 2.4 MW (~1 turbine) site. q50r 0.41 but q90r 0.99 (matches expected at high wind). Early-operation commissioning/tuning combined with winter icing on a single-turbine site - first-winter ramp-up artifact.
evidence_note: First winter post-COD on a 1-turbine site; q90r ~1.0 indicates only low-wind/icing shortfall.

potential_reason: Short autumn period, mild shortfall (q50r 0.60, q90r 0.83) on a single-turbine site. Brief turbine downtime/servicing would produce this; on a 1-turbine farm even a few days offline depresses the monthly ratio.
evidence_note: Single-turbine site; short outage dominates the ratio.

potential_reason: Winter-spring 2020, q50r 0.49 over ~5 months on a 1-turbine site. Sustained partial output suggests a derated/intermittently-faulted turbine plus winter icing. Extended turbine fault is the most likely driver on such a small site.
evidence_note: Long winter-spring shortfall on single turbine = fault/derate plus icing.

potential_reason: Winter period (q50r 0.43, q90r 0.85). Recurring cold-season shortfall on this small site; icing losses and/or winter maintenance downtime most likely. Recurs each winter, pointing to a seasonal (icing) effect rather than a one-off failure.
source_name: NVE - Norwegian wind power production data
evidence_note: Repeating winter pattern indicates seasonal icing/maintenance losses.

potential_reason: Another winter period (q50r 0.44, q90r 0.88). Consistent with the site's recurring cold-season under-production - icing losses dominating, output otherwise near-normal at high wind. Seasonal icing/availability effect.
source_name: NVE - Norwegian wind power production data
evidence_note: Repeated winter under-production = icing/seasonal losses.

potential_reason: Autumn 2025, q50r 0.42, q90r 0.67 on a 1-turbine site. Partial turbine availability / early-winter icing. On such a small site a single turbine derate or outage drives the ratio down.
evidence_note: Autumn shortfall on single-turbine site; outage or onset of icing.

potential_reason: Part of the Bjerkreim Southern Cluster commissioned June 12, 2020. This flag is ~3-4 months after COD (2020-07-14), with deep q50r 0.26 but q90r 0.86. The fleet of 70 SG turbines was still being commissioned/handed over through autumn 2020, so reduced online capacity vs full nameplate is the most likely cause - commissioning ramp-up artifact.
source_name: Global Energy Monitor / Valinor - Bjerkreim cluster commissioned June 2020
evidence_note: Bjerkreim cluster commissioned mid-2020; flag falls in the staged ramp-up window, q90r 0.86 indicates only partial fleet online.

potential_reason: Winter period, q50_ratio trigger only (q50r 0.35, q90r 0.84). Small 13.2 MW farm in the Fosen/Bessaker area; cold-season icing losses with output otherwise near-normal at high wind is the most likely cause.
source_name: NVE - Norwegian wind power production data
evidence_note: q90r 0.84 with low q50r = low-wind/icing shortfall in winter.

potential_reason: Winter 2023/24 (q50r 0.23, q90r 0.73). Recurring cold-season under-production at this coastal Trondelag site - icing losses and/or partial winter availability. Deeper than the 2017 winter flag, so some turbine downtime may also be involved.
source_name: NVE - Norwegian wind power production data
evidence_note: Repeating winter shortfall; icing/availability.

potential_reason: ~13-month span at q50r 0.39, q90r 0.70 on a small fleet. A year-long sustained shortfall most likely reflects a long-running turbine fault/derate (one of few turbines offline for an extended repair) rather than weather. A capacity/metering-baseline mismatch should also be checked given the duration.
source_name: ENTSO-E Transparency - Unavailability of Generation Units
evidence_note: Multi-month duration on small fleet = extended turbine outage; verify baseline too.

potential_reason: Single December month, q50_ratio trigger only (q50r 0.45, q90r 0.93 - runs near-normal at high wind). Tiny 6.9 MW Rogaland site; the high q90r points to a low-wind/icing seasonal shortfall rather than an outage. Winter icing losses.
source_name: NVE - Norwegian wind power production data
evidence_note: q90r 0.93 rules out outage; single-month December = icing/low-wind.

potential_reason: Summer 2025 shortfall (q50r 0.32, q90r 0.79) on the large 288 MW Fosen farm. Summer maintenance season with multiple of 80 turbines offline for servicing is the most likely cause. Fosen operated continuously through its Sami legal dispute, so curtailment is unlikely.
evidence_note: Summer service window; q90r 0.79 indicates operational (maintenance) cause, not grid cap.

potential_reason: Tiny 6.4 MW (~1-2 turbine) site only months after COD (2018-09-19). Very deep q50r 0.055 in winter = essentially no output. Early-operation fault / outage or first-winter commissioning issue on a single turbine. Could also be a metering gap.
evidence_note: Near-zero output first winter post-COD on tiny site = outage or commissioning/metering issue.

potential_reason: Spring 2021, q50r 0.47, q90r 0.50 (low at all wind speeds). A roughly flat ~50% deficit across wind speeds on a 1-2 turbine site suggests one of two turbines offline (halving output). Partial turbine unavailability.
evidence_note: ~50% flat deficit consistent with one of two turbines down.

potential_reason: 8-month span, q50r 0.41 / q90r 0.50 (flat ~half output). Sustained ~50% deficit across the whole wind range over many months strongly indicates one turbine of the small fleet offline for an extended period. Long-running turbine outage.
evidence_note: Multi-month flat half-output = extended single-turbine outage.

potential_reason: Single January month, q50r 0.50, q90r 0.84. Winter icing or brief turbine downtime on the small Karmoy site; output near-normal at high wind points to a low-wind/icing seasonal effect.
source_name: NVE - Norwegian wind power production data
evidence_note: Winter month, q90r 0.84 indicates icing/low-wind shortfall.

potential_reason: 7-month span at q50r 0.000, q90r 0.109 - essentially zero output for over half a year on a tiny site. Most likely the turbine(s) were permanently shut down / decommissioned, or the metering feed stopped reporting. Flag as decommissioning or data dropout; verify the site is still operational before treating as a real loss.
evidence_note: Sustained zero output H2-2025 = shutdown/decommissioning or metering gap; confirm operational status.

potential_reason: Summer-autumn 2022 mild shortfall (q50r 0.59, q90r 0.78) on a 25.2 MW Dalane (Egersund) site. Maintenance-season partial turbine availability is the most likely cause; modest magnitude argues against a major outage or grid cap.
source_name: ENTSO-E Transparency - Unavailability of Generation Units
evidence_note: Summer service-window partial availability; modest ratio.

potential_reason: Autumn 2023 deep shortfall (q50r 0.15, q90r 0.82). Sormarkfjellet (31x Vestas V117, operator Aneo) has a documented history of turbine-blade damage events leading to turbines being stopped and the park closed. While the most-publicised blade failures were Jan/Mar 2025, the autumn-2023 deep shortfall is consistent with an earlier turbine outage/inspection campaign; q90r 0.82 indicates the running turbines were fine. Partial fleet unavailability.

evidence_note: Operator Aneo documents repeated blade-damage turbine stops; 2023 autumn flag fits an earlier outage, though the named blade incidents are 2025.

potential_reason: Summer 2018, q50_ratio trigger only (q50r 0.39, q90r 0.77), on the 168 MW Tellenes farm less than a year after COD (2017-08-26). Summer maintenance/early-operation servicing with part of the fleet offline is the most likely cause. Possible residual commissioning effect.

evidence_note: First summer post-COD; q50-only trigger fits low-wind summer plus partial outage.

potential_reason: Autumn 2023, mild shortfall (q50r 0.56, q90r 0.66) on a tiny 10.35 MW (~3 turbine) Rogaland site. Partial turbine availability / brief outage; modest magnitude.
evidence_note: Small site, modest deficit = partial turbine downtime.

potential_reason: 7-month span, q50r 0.53, q90r 0.66 (roughly flat partial deficit). On a ~3-turbine site a sustained ~half-output deficit over many months suggests one turbine offline long-term. Extended turbine outage; check for a baseline/capacity mismatch given the duration.
evidence_note: Multi-month flat partial deficit on tiny fleet = long single-turbine outage.

potential_reason: Autumn 2025, mild shortfall (q50r 0.66, q90r 0.85) on the 47.3 MW Tysvaer farm (11x SG 4.3 MW). Modest deficit with near-normal high-wind output points to onset of winter icing and/or minor maintenance downtime rather than a major event.
source_name: Global Energy Monitor - Tysvaer wind farm
evidence_note: Mild autumn deficit; icing/minor maintenance. No specific 2025 incident found.

potential_reason: Utsira (1.2 MW, 2 Enercon turbines on an island off Haugesund) was a wind/hydrogen demonstration site with chronically low/erratic availability and ~600 kW units feeding an isolated island system. Its 15 flags spanning 2011-2025 at persistently low q50r are characteristic of a small demonstration plant with frequent downtime and likely metering/baseline mismatch against the 1.2 MW nameplate. This winter flag is one such period - data artifact / demonstration-site availability.
source_name: ScienceDirect / IFE - Utsira wind-hydrogen demonstration system
evidence_note: Documented R&D demonstration plant on an isolated island grid with limited availability; pattern across 15 flags fits a demo site, not normal commercial production.

potential_reason: Winter 2011, q50r 0.25 on the Utsira demonstration site. Same pattern as the other Utsira flags: small island demo plant with frequent downtime / baseline mismatch. Likely a data/availability artifact rather than a discrete loss event.
evidence_note: Demonstration plant with low/erratic availability; part of a 15-flag chronic-shortfall pattern.

potential_reason: Spring 2012, q50r 0.31. Recurring chronic under-production at the Utsira demo site; availability/metering artifact most likely.
evidence_note: Chronic low-output demonstration site; recurring artifact.

potential_reason: Winter 2013/14, q50r 0.44. Continuation of Utsira's chronic low-availability pattern; small demonstration plant with frequent downtime and likely capacity-baseline mismatch.
evidence_note: Demo-site chronic shortfall; artifact.

evidence_note: Recurring demo-site shortfall; artifact.

potential_reason: Winter 2016, very deep q50r 0.08, q90r 0.48 - near-total loss. On a 2-turbine demo site this indicates the turbine(s) were largely offline (extended fault/decommissioning of the demonstration units). Could also be a metering gap. Most likely a major outage/shutdown of the demo plant.
evidence_note: Near-zero output = extended turbine outage or demo-plant shutdown.

potential_reason: Winter 2019, q50r 0.41. Utsira demonstration-site chronic shortfall; availability/baseline artifact.
evidence_note: Recurring demo-site shortfall; artifact.

potential_reason: 8-month span, q50r 0.24, q90r 0.41 - sustained heavy under-production through most of 2020 on the 2-turbine demo site. Long-running turbine outage / partial decommissioning most likely. Possible metering issue given the duration.
evidence_note: 8-month deep shortfall = extended outage of the demo turbines.

potential_reason: March 2021, deep q50r 0.12, q90r 0.66. One turbine of the pair offline / fault on the demo site. Partial turbine unavailability.

evidence_note: Recurring demo-site shortfall; artifact.

potential_reason: September 2023, deep q50r 0.18, q90r 0.46. Turbine(s) largely offline on the demo site. Outage / fault.
evidence_note: Deep monthly shortfall = turbine offline.

potential_reason: ~8-month span, q50r 0.25, q90r 0.49. Prolonged heavy under-production - extended turbine outage / advanced degradation of the aging demonstration units (site dates to ~2004/2010). Long-running outage; verify against decommissioning.
evidence_note: Multi-month deep shortfall on aging demo plant = extended outage.

potential_reason: Winter 2024/25, ~6 months, q50r 0.41, q90r 0.54. Continuation of Utsira's chronic low-output pattern; demonstration-plant availability/baseline artifact, possibly intermittent operation late in the site's life.

potential_reason: Autumn 2025, q50r 0.000, q90r 0.33 - essentially no output. Most likely the aging demonstration turbines were finally shut down / decommissioned, or the data feed stopped. Decommissioning or data dropout; confirm operational status.

potential_reason: Flag begins ~2 months after COD (2006-10-27) on this early Norwegian wind site. q50r 0.57 in the first winter - early-operation commissioning/tuning of the original Valsneset turbines. First-winter ramp-up.

Its 17 flags (2006-2025) at persistently low q50r (~0.2-0.4) across all seasons are characteristic of a test field with prototype/temporary turbines and capacity-baseline mismatch rather than a normally-operating commercial farm. This summer 2007 flag is one such period.

potential_reason: Vardafjell (Sandnes) was the subject of a high-profile noise dispute: Sandnes municipality imposed a strict Lden 42 dBA limit (Aug 2020) requiring the operator to run in NOISE-REDUCED mode. This flag (q50r 0.000 over 4 winter months) coincides exactly with that mandated reduced-operation/shutdown period - i.e. regulatory curtailment, not a wind or hardware fault. (Categorised as curtailment; effectively a regulatory operating restriction.)

evidence_note: Documented noise-limit ruling forcing reduced/halted operation in 2020-2021; matches the q50r=0.000 winter window.

potential_reason: Summer 2021, deep q50r 0.11, q90r 0.42 - heavy ongoing under-production. Continuation of the regulatory noise-curtailment regime at Vardafjell (operator required to run in reduced mode pending appeals). Regulatory operating restriction; turbine outage also possible.
source_name: Statsforvalteren i Rogaland - Vardafjell noise complaint ruling
evidence_note: Noise-reduced operation continued through 2021 appeals; consistent with the deep summer shortfall.

potential_reason: The flag window (2002-2012, ~52,779 h) STARTS A DECADE BEFORE the registered COD (2012-09-15) and shows q50r 0.016 / q90r 0.038 (essentially zero). This is a clear data artifact: pre-COD timestamps with near-zero output, almost certainly a date/parsing error or pre-operational period erroneously included in the series. False positive - exclude.
source_name: NVE wind power plant register (Ytre Vikna)
evidence_note: Flag spans 2002-2012, entirely before the 2012-09 COD, at ~0 output = pre-operational/date-error artifact, not a real loss.

potential_reason: Autumn 2025, q50r 0.50, q90r 0.67 on the 39.1 MW Ytre Vikna farm. Moderate deficit consistent with onset of winter icing and/or partial turbine maintenance on this exposed coastal Namdalen site (64.9N). Seasonal/maintenance availability.
source_name: ENTSO-E Transparency - Unavailability of Generation Units
evidence_note: Moderate autumn deficit; icing/partial maintenance. No specific incident found.

potential_reason: Tiny 1.6 MW (~1 turbine) site. Summer-autumn 2015, deep q50r 0.21 but q90r 0.79 (near-normal at high wind). On a single-turbine site, intermittent downtime / fault during the maintenance season drives this. Partial turbine availability.
evidence_note: 1-turbine site; q90r 0.79 indicates downtime, not weather alone.

potential_reason: Late 2018, q50r 0.44, q90r 0.76 on the 1-turbine site. Winter low-wind/icing shortfall and/or brief downtime. Seasonal effect.
source_name: NVE - Norwegian wind power production data
evidence_note: Winter shortfall on single turbine; icing/minor downtime.

potential_reason: Late summer-autumn 2020, q50r 0.23, q90r 0.52 on the 1-turbine site. Sustained deep deficit over 3 months suggests the turbine was offline/faulted for an extended part of the period. Turbine outage.
evidence_note: 3-month deep deficit on single turbine = extended outage.

potential_reason: ~8-month span, q50r 0.20, q90r 0.46 on a 1-turbine site. Prolonged heavy under-production across most of 2025 indicates the turbine was largely offline - extended outage or end-of-life shutdown/decommissioning. Verify operational status; possible metering gap.
evidence_note: 8-month deep deficit on single turbine = extended outage / possible decommissioning.
